title: The Midnight Campfire
text: We were up in the Cascades, miles from any cell service. Around 2:00 AM, the entire forest went silent—no crickets, no wind. Suddenly, our campfire turned a bright, neon green. A tall figure, maybe seven feet, walked right through our campsite. It didn't look at us; it was carrying a metallic box that hummed like a microwave. It walked straight into a solid rock face and just... merged with it. The fire turned back to orange immediately after.

title: The Reflection in the Lake
text: I was out on the water at dusk. The lake was like a mirror. I looked down at the reflection of the clouds, but the reflection didn't match the sky. In the water, I could see a massive city with glowing towers and flying lights, right where the bottom of the lake should be. I reached down to touch it, and a hand—with way too many fingers—reached up from the 'sky' in the water to meet mine. I paddled back to shore and haven't gone near the water since.

title: The Underwater Lantern
text: We were night-fishing about 12 miles off the coast. Something huge passed under the boat. It wasn't a whale; it was glowing with a bioluminescence I’ve never seen. It was shaped like a massive gear. As it passed, our sonar screen went haywire, showing 'terrain' at 50 feet deep where the charts say it's 3,000. It rose to the surface a mile away and just... shot into the clouds without making a splash.

"Somewhere over the Atlantic, I looked out the window. There was a craft flying wing-tip to wing-tip with our Boeing 747. It looked like molten glass, constantly changing shape. I tried to wake up the guy sleeping next to me, but he wouldn't move—nobody in the cabin would move. It was like time had frozen for everyone but me. The pilot’s voice came over the intercom, but it wasn't English. It sounded like rhythmic clicking."
title: The "Red Eye" Flight

text:
Click me

"I was the only one in the last subway car on the Blue Line. The train stopped between stations, which happens all the time. But when the doors opened into the dark tunnel, a man stepped in. He was wearing a suit that looked like it was made of liquid mercury. He sat across from me and opened a newspaper—except the 'paper' was glowing and the text was moving like a liquid. He got off at the next stop and disappeared before the doors even closed."
title: "The Mercury Man"
The Attic Signal
text: "I was testing a vintage shortwave radio I bought at a flea market. I started picking up a signal that sounded like a heavy heartbeat. Then, my smart bulbs started changing colors to match the beat. I looked out the attic window and saw a massive, flat disk hovering just above the power lines. It was 'drinking' the electricity—I could see blue sparks jumping from the transformer up to the craft. My whole block was in a blackout for three hours, but the power company said there was 'no recorded fault'."
